content.text: Overview
This page explains the basic configuration for Schedule table
Configuration
Each schedule table needs to be assigned a unique name so that it can be referred to in API calls

items: As in overview page, The schedule table works on OSEK Counter, so we need to select the OSEK counter reference
schedule table has two modes of operation:

content.text: Below configuration example is a Schedule table named ScheduleTable_Test with duration of 100 ms (The tick counter is in ms)
Synchronization Strategy

Expiry points occur at the same relative offsets from the notional zero each time the table repeats However, the counter tick value at which expiry points occur depends on when the schedule table was started.

The absolute time at which the Initial Expiry Point on a schedule table is processed is under user control. However, if the schedule table repeats then it is not guaranteed that the absolute count value at which the initial expiry point was first processed is the same count value at which it is subsequently processed. This is because the duration of the schedule table need not be equal to the counter modulus
In many cases it may be important that schedule table expiry points are processed at specific absolute values of the underlying counter. This is called synchronization.
Typical use-cases include

items: Synchronization of expiry points to degrees of angular rotation for motor management
Synchronizing the computation to a global (network) time base

content.text: Types of Synchronization

items: Implicit - assumes that the counter used to drive the schedule table is the counter with which synchronization is required
Explicit assumes that there are two counters

content.text: Auto-Start
We can configure that the Schedule table has to be auto started by RTA-OS
Expiry Point configuration
We have following configuration for expiry points
Expiry Point offset - The offset from the start (the notional zero) of the schedule table
Expiry Point name - Can be configured with any meaningful name
Max Shorten (Retard) - defines the maximum number of ticks that can be subtracted from the expiry point’s offset
This is equivalent to shortening the delay from the previous expiry point by the specified number of ticks
Max Lengthen (Advance) - defines the maximum number of ticks that can be added to the expiry point’s offset
This is equivalent to lengthening the delay from the previous expiry point by the specified number of ticks
Now each Expiry point can activate N number of Tasks and/or set N number of events
Action Name: Configure meaningful Action name
Action Type: Activate Task or Set Event
Task Name: If Action type is selected as Activate Task → Choose the task which has to be activated from drop down list
Event Name: If Action type is selected as Set Event → Choose the event which has to be set from drop down list
Applications
Select Owning application and accessible application in Application Configuration tab
